title: Quit planner - Canada.ca
content:
- How to use this quit planner

## How to use quit planner
A quit plan is a set of steps you can use to prepare for your next attempt to quit smoking or quit vaping. Making a quit plan and putting it into action can make quitting easier and help you succeed.
Answer the questions below to make your quit plan.
You can write out your answers on a piece of paper, or type your answers into the boxes below.
Please note that we do not collect or save your answers. When you click submit you can:
- Print your customized quit plan
- Save it as a screen shot
- Copy and paste it into a document
Choosing tools to  help you quit such as long-acting nicotine replacement therapy or medications  can reduce the frequency and severity of cravings. Even though NRT is available  without a prescription, these products are not recommended for anyone under 18  years of age, unless recommended by their doctor.
Return to footnote 1 referrer
Unlike nicotine  replacement therapy (NRT), vaping products have not been approved as quit  smoking aids in Canada under the Foods and Drugs Act . However, adults  have legal access to these commercial products as a less harmful alternative to  continuing to smoke.
Public Health Consequences of E-Cigarettes
Return to footnote 2 referrer
Cytisine is approved in Canada as a natural health product for smoking cessation. It does not require a prescription and is sold in certain pharmacies, health stores, and online.
Return to footnote 3 referrer
